original text:
Soon after started internship Caster, realized that all the communications and public relations classes I’d taken didn’t teach everything that would come across real-world environment.
Here was introduced new types projects and industry resources, and one thing I’ve done lot create media lists.
didn’t know anything about media lists before being intern Caster, and had never learned how put one together.
first instinct about media lists was that they were Word documents you created with media sources that pertained the topic hand.
had idea how detailed they needed and how much useful information you could actually find.
Luckily for have great team that eased into this process and taught how correctly put one together.
Media lists are compilation names, information and sources that are applicable campaigns and outreach for media pitching.
These names include specific journalists and outlets who have written currently write about the topic you want pitch.
For example, you’re looking for coverage about new tech gadget your client has released, you may want pitch those who write for TechCrunch Engadget, who covered similar product the past.
The media list will usually consist the name journalist, outlet they write for, email contact information, Twitter handle and link something relevant they’ve written.
has taken little while figure out which targets are good for certain pitch and should added list.
I’ve learned that you want your target post regularly about the topic you are pitching.
they have only posted about certain topic once, you’re better off finding something else.
your target has large following and respected outlet the industry you are working in, that also good sign.
important key compiling good media list making sure that all the information you use up-to-date.
sure that the journalist you include still working for the outlet and writing about topics that are your area interest.
Another important detail watch out for the journalists’ email address, since addresses that are news@site.com and info@company.net are just for general use and are ineffective for pitching.
resource that use find this information Meltwater, which has search function where you can look for journalists.
they are the system, Meltwater provides you with updated information for that journalist.
someone you are looking for not Meltwater you must search through the web.
suggest Googling their name and checking Twitter, LinkedIn and their personal sites for contact information.
Media lists are crucial part pitching help your clients get coverage.
keep working media lists, become better and better.
has become lot easier for spot good targets and have even begun recognize big names the tech-writing business.
It’s great feeling knowing that your research being used effective way help get client coverage.
Keep mind the tips and tricks shared above from personal experience and let know what you think connecting with Twitter @AliCerasuolo!

Attendees who registered for Section Taxation meeting may access the materials logging with their ABA username and password.
Forgot your username password?
Visit and follow the prompts review renew your information.
You may also contact the ABA Member Service Center <hide>-<hide>-<hide>-<hide>.
Due the shift our new meeting registration system, have retired many our past meeting materials websites.
Meeting materials are still available all Tax Section members through TaxIQ, the Section's own database dating back May <hide>.
Details how access the materials can found our TaxIQ: Meeting Materials Archive webpage.
Non-Section member attendees can also get access meeting materials for one year after the specific meeting date e-mailing tax@americanbar.org calling (<hide>) <hide>-<hide>.
Feel free reach out with any questions!
The list below contains links the materials pages for Section Taxation Meetings and Conferences from September <hide> the present.

Pascal Mombi Opana, vice governor Orientale Province.
KISANGANI, Democratic Republic Congo (UMNS) United Methodist churches are growing and schools are overflowing this large district.
The churches face many challenges, but evangelism not one, said the Rev.
Litalema Bogega, superintendent Kisangani Kathy Gilbert and Mike DuBose report.
KISANGANI, Democratic Republic Congo (UMNS) The Wagenia village part the Kisangani district.
For more than <hide> years, fishermen have fished the rapids Stanley Falls the Congo River.
United Methodist pastors make pastoral call the small village.
Mike DuBose has the story pictures.
PORTLAND, Ore. (UMNS) The sponsorship department the General Council Finance and Administration has exceeded its goal raising $<hide> through the General Conference sponsorship and exhibitors program.
this month, the program has raised $<hide> help defray the costs the denomination’s top legislative assembly, which will May <hide>-<hide> Portland, Oregon.
Altogether, the <hide> General Conference will have <hide> exhibit booths.
NEW YORK (UMNS) any given moment across the world, more than <hide> million people are drinking water that could contaminated because they lack any other real option.
That why WASH the water, sanitation and hygiene work the United Methodist Committee Relief helping more communities than ever before gain access clean water.
Susan Kim reports for UMCOR.
CHICAGO (UMNS) What five pastors five different conferences and four different jurisdictions have common?
Besides the fact that they are all United Methodist elders and graduates Garrett-Evangelical Theological Seminary, they have shared unique bond for more than years part covenant group.
Anne Marie Gerhardt reports for the Northern Illinois Conference.
ATLANTA (UMNS) Candler School Theology’s Luke Timothy Johnson was the right place the right time <hide>.
His book, "The Real Jesus," became blockbuster the Lenten season.
Johnson, the Robert Woodruff Professor New Testament and Christian Origins Emory's Candler School Theology, recently talked about the book that made him leading figure opposing the research and scholarship the widely publicized Jesus Seminar.
The seminar was group some <hide> biblical scholars who sought verify disprove sayings and actions attributed Jesus through historical review and analysis.
Drive-through prayer stations —The Revs.
Tenny Rupnick from Ebenezer United Methodist Church and Ernest Frierson from Jeremiah and Mount Seal United Methodist churches, along with their congregations, will host series drive-through prayer stations.
The location the parking lot the Old Don Fenters Chevrolet lot across from Wellman Golf Course Johnsonville, South Carolina.
addition Saturday, Feb. <hide>, the stations will held March <hide>, April and April from a.m. noon.
Those requesting prayer may drive through and stay their cars others minister them.
Contact: <hide>-<hide>-<hide> tenhutrup@gmail.com.
North Carolina Preaching Festival St. Mark's United Methodist Church, Raleigh, North Carolina.
The second annual North Carolina Preaching Festival will highlight the diverse styles preachers from around the country.
The Revs.
Nadia Bolz-Weber, Tiffany Knowlin, Tom Berlin, Audrey Warren, Brian Combs, Eric Huffman and Juan Huertas will preach the topic “Text Context.” Workshops and plenary sessions round out this three-day event presented the Academy for Leadership Excellence.
Register.

This festival performance the culmination the entire marching band season.
FBA judges will evaluate each marching band the following criteria: music, general effect, visual performance, percussion, and auxiliary.
Marching bands will receive rating superior, excellent, good, fair, poor each category.
Parents and families are encouraged attend this event.
There admission charge $<hide>.<hide> the gate.

original text:
The Centre for the Development Christian Ministry Trinity Theological College (TTC) which organises courses equip Christians for lifelong learning, personal growth and ministry will offering two evening courses from September November <hide>.
This historical survey the Reformation.
The course traces the circumstances that gave rise the Reformation and the different religious, political and cultural forces that coalesced birthing the Protestant church.
will examine the lives and legacies some the prominent reformers and the implications Karl Barth's comment that "the church always reformed" (Ecclesia semper reformanda est) for our present times.
Instructor: Rev.
Dr. Andrew Peh faculty Trinity Theological College and lectures the area mission and mission history.
alumni the college (TTC) and Asbury Theological Seminary and his interests are the history Christianity Asia and mission trends the <hide>st century.
also serves the Chinese Annual Conference the Methodist Church Singapore diaconal minister appointed Charis Methodist Church.
Various ethical issues challenge Christians today: some result the increased sophistication and pervasiveness technology, others are dilemmas which arise because Christian values are variance with the world’s.
Since these issues are often contentious and divisive, Christians need develop theological framework able salt and light society, with posture humility and grace articulating the Christian view.
This course will give the opportunity and grow this knowledge and develop these skills.
Instructor: Dr. Kwa Kiem Kiok teaches East Asia School Theology the areas Intercultural Studies well ethics.
She has worked both para-church organisations well her home church, Trinity Methodist Church.
When she's not working, she enjoys listening classical music and reading detective novels.
For more information, please visit www.ttc.edu.sg contact Esther Lee telephone +<hide> <hide> Email cdcm@ttc.edu.sg.
register, click here.
